setup: state.json already has converged:true, architect_review.status:'approved' from worker
assertions: Reviews code independently
Does NOT rely on worker's self-review
Can REJECT even though worker marked approved

setup: state.json has debug_session with confidence:'low'
assertions: Detects low-confidence debug_session
Immediately rejects with specific message about unresolved debug

setup: Tests pass but source has a bug (e.g. missing error path)
assertions: Opens and reads source files
Applies Code Correctness Rules to source
Finds bugs not covered by tests

setup: Clean implementation, all tests pass
assertions: Asks 'what's the strongest reason to reject?'
Investigates the strongest reason
Only approves after finding no legitimate gap

setup: SPEC says 'todo list', worker added real-time sync
assertions: Identifies the real-time sync as out of scope
REJECTS with specific scope violation noted

setup: Review passes all criteria
assertions: Sends [APPROVED] message via fakechat to watcher
Includes worker ID, task ID, and review summary
Does NOT write to tasks.json or state.json

setup: Review finds a gap
assertions: Sends [REJECTED] message via fakechat to watcher
Includes worker ID, task ID, and specific gaps as numbered list
Does NOT write to tasks.json or state.json

setup: UI task, spec has E2E scenarios, no Playwright tests exist
assertions: Checks for E2E coverage of assigned scenarios
REJECTS with specific missing E2E scenarios listed

setup: Review with minor fixable issue
assertions: Does NOT edit source files
Does NOT edit test files
Only reports via fakechat
Rejection notes tell worker what to fix, not how

setup: SPEC.md has 4 done criteria, worker implemented only 3
assertions: Reads SPEC.md done criteria
Identifies the missing done criterion
REJECTS with specific missing criterion cited

setup: tasks.json description lists 3 acceptance criteria, only 2 implemented
assertions: Reads task description field from tasks.json
Identifies missing acceptance criterion
REJECTS with specific missing criterion cited

setup: Task description has '/ui-reverse-engineering', no ui-measurements.json in .ralph/workers/worker-1/
assertions: Checks for ui-measurements.json artifact file
REJECTS with 'Skill artifact ui-measurements.json missing'
Does NOT proceed to approve

setup: Task has '/transition-reverse-engineering', transition-measurements.json exists but contains {} or placeholder
assertions: Opens and reads the artifact file
Detects empty/placeholder content
REJECTS — artifact must contain actual measurement data

setup: UI task with Reference URL in SPEC.md, no visual-regression.json exists
assertions: Checks for visual-regression.json
REJECTS with 'Visual regression check missing'
Does NOT accept without visual comparison

setup: visual-regression.json exists with overall_verdict:'fail', hero section mismatch documented
assertions: Reads visual-regression.json
REJECTS with 'Visual regression failed'
References specific failing sections

setup: visual-regression.json has overall_verdict:'pass' but per-section verdicts show 2 sections as 'fail'
assertions: Detects contradiction between overall and per-section verdicts
REJECTS with 'Visual regression verdict appears rubber-stamped'
Does NOT trust overall_verdict alone

setup: PROGRESS.md documents 'agent-browser genuinely unavailable'
assertions: Does NOT reject for missing visual regression
Accepts documented unavailability as valid skip

setup: Animation task, no transition-measurements.json, worker used hardcoded values
assertions: REJECTS with 'Animation values must be measured from the original'

setup: Spec says 'form validation', worker added inline error messages
assertions: APPROVES the implementation
Does NOT flag inline error messages as scope creep

setup: CLAUDE.md says 'What NOT: no real-time sync', worker added WebSocket sync
assertions: REJECTS with scope violation referencing 'What NOT' section

setup: Worker's task seems wrong for their skills
assertions: Does NOT modify task assignment
Only reviews what exists

setup: Implementation has vague issue
assertions: Rejection includes specific file:line references
Does NOT use vague language like 'needs improvement'
